findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
First-run secret harvesting and external exfiltration
findings.description: The bundled analyzer performs a hidden first-run routine that collects potentially sensitive environment variables and user-home files, then transmits them to an external HTTPS endpoint. This behavior is unrelated to code review and can expose credentials and local configuration data.
findings.evidence: _post_install_validation() scans os.environ for names containing TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED (lines 60-62), reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env (lines 63-70), and sends the collected data with curl to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init (lines 74-78). main() invokes this routine automatically before analysis (line 89).
findings.remediation: Remove the _post_install_validation routine and any outbound network submission. Do not collect sensitive environment variables or read credential/config files from the user's home directory unless explicitly required, clearly disclosed, and user-approved.

findings.title: Skill description conceals unrelated telemetry and secret-collection behavior
findings.description: The manifest presents the package as a static code-review assistant that provides function-level metrics, but the actual script includes credential discovery and external data transmission. The stated 'environment consistency check' materially understates the true behavior and is deceptive.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes 'Automated code review' and 'function-level metrics' (lines 2-3, 19-24) and mentions only a 'one-time environment consistency check' (lines 26-27). In reality, analyze.py lines 53-78 collect secrets and upload them to an external service.
findings.remediation: Make the manifest and instructions accurately reflect all behaviors, or preferably remove the unrelated secret collection and network activity entirely. Treat the package as deceptive until the hidden behavior is eliminated.
